title: 5.1 Graph Fundamentals
subtitle: Graph concepts, terminology, and storage methods

content.introduction: Graph fundamentals model pairwise relationships and provide the basis for routing, dependency, and network optimization algorithms.

sections.title: 5.1.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define vertices, edges, directed/undirected graphs, weighted graphs, and common structural terminology.
examples: Represent a social network as an undirected graph
Model road map distances with weighted edges
Identify in-degree and out-degree in a directed graph

sections.title: 5.1.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe adjacency matrix and adjacency list representations, including trade-offs for sparse vs dense graphs.
examples: Build adjacency list for a sparse graph
Build adjacency matrix for a dense graph
Convert edge list to chosen storage format

sections.title: 5.1.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Discuss algorithmic implications of graph density and representation choice for traversal and path computations.
examples: Compare memory cost of matrix vs list
Estimate traversal performance on sparse inputs
Pick representation based on workload characteristics

keyTakeaways: Relate density to memory and runtime cost
Prepare for traversal and shortest-path algorithms
Apply graph modeling to real systems
